title: Bash Scripting Cheat Sheet
description: Essential Bash scripting commands and patterns for engineers.

commands.description: Lists files in the current directory
commands.scenario: Lists files in the current directory

commands.description: Assigns 'Hello' to a variable named greeting
commands.scenario: Assigns 'Hello' to a variable named greeting

commands.description: Prints the value of the variable greeting
commands.scenario: Prints the value of the variable greeting

commands.description: Passes a script argument to a variable
commands.scenario: Passes a script argument to a variable

commands.command: if [ -f "file.txt" ]; then echo "File exists"; else echo "File does not exist"; fi
commands.description: If-else statement to check if a file exists
commands.scenario: If-else statement to check if a file exists

commands.command: for i in {1..5}; do echo "Number $i"; done
commands.description: For loop iterating over a list of numbers
commands.scenario: For loop iterating over a list of numbers

commands.command: i=1; while [ $i -le 5 ]; do echo $i; i=$((i + 1)); done
commands.description: While loop that prints numbers from 1 to 5
commands.scenario: While loop that prints numbers from 1 to 5

commands.description: Defines a function that prints 'Hello, World!'
commands.scenario: Defines a function that prints 'Hello, World!'

commands.description: Function with parameters that adds two numbers
commands.scenario: Function with parameters that adds two numbers

commands.description: Creates a new file named newfile.txt
commands.scenario: Creates a new file named newfile.txt

commands.description: Appends 'Hello' to a file named greetings.txt
commands.scenario: Appends 'Hello' to a file named greetings.txt

commands.command: while IFS= read -r line; do echo "$line"; done < file.txt
commands.description: Reads a file line by line
commands.scenario: Reads a file line by line
